meanings.definition: the act of creating something by casting it in a mold
the act of throwing a fishing line out over the water by means of a rod and reel
the choice of actors to play particular roles in a play or movie

examples: She threw the flashlight beam into the corner
The setting sun threw long shadows

meanings.definition: select to play,sing, or dance a part in a play, movie, musical, opera, or ballet
examples: He cast a young woman in the role of Desdemona

meanings.definition: assign the roles of (a movie or a play) to actors
move about aimlessly or without any destination, often in search of food or employment
examples: The cattle roam across the prairie
the laborers drift from one town to the next
They rolled from town to town

meanings.definition: form by pouring (e.g., wax or hot metal) into a cast or mold

examples: he shed his image as a pushy boss

meanings.definition: formulate in a particular style or language
examples: I wouldn't put it that way
She cast her request in very polite language

meanings.definition: eject the contents of the stomach through the mouth
examples: After drinking too much, the students vomited
The patient regurgitated the food we gave him last night
